Question ID: architecture-derived-B5-combinatorial-probability-random-chords
Title: B5. Combinatorial Probability (Random Chords)

Problem:
Disk divided into 4 quadrants.
Add 25 random chords with endpoints in different quadrants.
What is the expected number of regions?

Grading target:
Exact numerical match.

Title: B5. Combinatorial Probability (Random Chords)

Problem:

Node started: Calculate the probability that two random chords intersect, given that the endpoints of each chord must lie in different quadrants of a disk divided into 4 equal quadrants. Assume the endpoints are chosen uniformly and independently from the circumference within their respective quadrants.

Node started: Given the probability p that any two chords intersect, calculate the expected number of intersection points among 25 chords. Then, use the formula for the expected number of regions: 1 + (number of chords) + (expected number of intersection points).
